metadata.description: Desktop Compose app UI automation flows with recording at three speed modes (slow, normal, fast)

challenges.name: Desktop: App Cold Start and Initial Render
challenges.description: Launch the Compose Desktop app from a cold state, verify the main window renders with the file browser panel, editor area, and toolbar within acceptable time bounds at each speed mode.

steps.description: Launch the Yole desktop application JAR via the JVM
Wait for the main window titled 'Yole' to appear on screen
Verify the window title bar displays 'Yole'
Verify all four navigation buttons are present in the sidebar
Verify the file browser panel is rendered on the left side
Verify the main editor area is rendered on the right side
Verify the top toolbar with action buttons is rendered
Capture a screenshot of the initial app state after cold start
Verify the File Browser is the default active screen on launch
Verify the cold start completed within the 8-second time budget

outputs.name: app_launched
outputs.description: Whether the app window appeared

outputs.name: main_window_rendered
outputs.description: Whether all main UI panels rendered

outputs.name: cold_start_time_ms
outputs.description: Time from launch to full render

assertions.message: Desktop app must launch successfully
Main window must render all panels
Cold start must complete within 8 seconds

challenges.name: Desktop: File Browser Navigation
challenges.description: Create a new folder in the file browser, navigate into it, navigate back out, and verify the breadcrumb trail and folder listing update correctly at each speed mode.

steps.description: Confirm the file browser panel is visible before starting navigation test
Click the file browser panel to ensure it has focus
Right-click inside the file browser to open the context menu
Verify the context menu appeared with folder creation option
Click 'New Folder' in the context menu to start folder creation
Type the folder name into the folder name input dialog
Press Enter to confirm the new folder creation
Wait for the file browser to refresh and show the new folder
Verify the newly created folder appears in the file listing
Double-click the new folder to navigate into it
Verify the breadcrumb trail shows the current folder name
Capture a screenshot showing navigation inside the new folder
Click the parent breadcrumb to navigate back up one level
Verify the folder is still visible in the parent directory listing

outputs.name: folder_created
outputs.description: Whether the new folder appeared in the listing

outputs.name: navigation_in
outputs.description: Whether navigating into the folder succeeded

outputs.name: navigation_out
outputs.description: Whether navigating back to parent succeeded

assertions.message: New folder must appear in file browser
Must navigate into folder and update breadcrumb
Must navigate back to parent directory

challenges.name: Desktop: Create Markdown Document
challenges.description: Create a new Markdown document, type real content including headings, bold text, a list, and a code block. Save the file and verify the content persists across a reload.

steps.description: Press Ctrl+N to open the new file dialog
Verify the new file dialog appeared
Type the filename with .md extension into the dialog
Press Enter to confirm file creation
Wait for the editor area to become active with an empty document
Type the Markdown heading on the first line
Press Enter twice to create a blank line after the heading
Type a paragraph with bold Markdown syntax
Press Enter twice to create a new paragraph

steps.value:
- Item one
- Item two
- Item three
steps.description: Type a three-item unordered Markdown list
Press Enter twice after the list
Type a Kotlin fenced code block
Press Ctrl+S to save the document
Wait for the save indicator to confirm the file was written to disk
Capture a screenshot of the editor with Markdown content
Close the current editor tab to simulate re-opening later
Double-click the file in the file browser to reopen it
Verify the persisted content contains the heading after reopening

outputs.name: file_created
outputs.description: Whether the new Markdown file was created

outputs.name: content_saved
outputs.description: Whether the content was saved successfully

outputs.name: content_persisted
outputs.description: Whether content survived close and reopen

assertions.message: New Markdown file must be created
Content must be saved to disk
Content must persist after close and reopen

challenges.name: Desktop: Create Todo.txt Document
challenges.description: Create a new todo.txt file, enter tasks with priorities, projects, and contexts. Verify task parsing, priority highlighting, and sort order.

steps.description: Press Ctrl+N to open the new file dialog
Verify the new file creation dialog appeared
Type 'tasks.todo' as the filename
Press Enter to confirm file creation with the .todo extension
Wait for the empty editor to become active with todo.txt format detected
Type a priority-A task with project, context, and due date
Press Enter to move to the next line
Type a priority-B task with project and context tags
Press Enter to move to the next line
Type a priority-C task
Press Enter to move to the next line
Type a completed task with completion date and the 'x' prefix
Press Ctrl+S to save the todo.txt file
Verify priority (A) task is highlighted with the highest priority color
Verify priority (B) task is highlighted with medium priority color
Verify the +yole project tag is syntax-highlighted
Verify the @dev context tag is syntax-highlighted
Verify the completed task is shown with strikethrough or dimmed styling
Capture a screenshot of the todo.txt editor with parsed and highlighted tasks

outputs.name: file_created
outputs.description: Whether the todo.txt file was created

outputs.name: tasks_parsed
outputs.description: Whether tasks were parsed with priorities and tags

outputs.name: completion_tracked
outputs.description: Whether completed task was marked correctly

assertions.message: Todo.txt file must be created
Tasks must be parsed with priorities, projects, and contexts
Completed task must show completion indicator

challenges.name: Desktop: Edit Existing Document
challenges.description: Open an existing document from the file browser, modify content by adding and removing lines, save changes, and verify modifications persist.

steps.description: Double-click the test file in the file browser to open it in the editor
Wait for the file content to fully load in the editor area
Verify the editor contains the expected pre-existing content
Capture a screenshot of the document before any edits
Click on line 3 of the editor to position the cursor there
Press Home then Shift+End to select the entire line 3 content

steps.value: This line was modified by the automation test.
steps.description: Type replacement text to overwrite the selected line
Press End then Enter to create a new line after the modified text

steps.value: This is a newly inserted line.
steps.description: Type a brand new line of text to insert into the document
Press Ctrl+S to save all changes to disk
Wait for the save indicator to confirm successful write
Verify the editor contains the modified line text
Verify the editor contains the newly inserted line
Capture a screenshot of the document after editing

outputs.name: file_opened
outputs.description: Whether the file opened in the editor

outputs.name: content_modified
outputs.description: Whether content was successfully modified

outputs.name: changes_saved
outputs.description: Whether changes were saved to disk

assertions.message: Existing file must open in the editor
Content must be editable
Changes must persist after save

challenges.name: Desktop: Theme Switching (Light, Dark, System)
challenges.description: Switch between Light, Dark, and System themes via the settings panel. Verify background color, text color, and syntax highlighting colors change at each switch.

steps.description: Click the Settings navigation button to open the settings panel
Verify the settings panel rendered with theme options
Click the theme selector dropdown to reveal theme options
Select 'Light' from the theme dropdown
Wait for the theme transition animation to complete
Verify the editor background changed to a light color scheme
Capture a screenshot showing the Light theme applied
Click the theme selector dropdown again
Select 'Dark' from the theme dropdown
Wait for the dark theme transition to complete
Verify the editor background changed to a dark color scheme
Capture a screenshot showing the Dark theme applied
Click the theme selector dropdown once more
Select 'System' to follow the OS theme preference
Verify the editor background matches the current OS theme setting

outputs.name: light_theme_applied
outputs.description: Whether light theme was applied correctly

outputs.name: dark_theme_applied
outputs.description: Whether dark theme was applied correctly

outputs.name: system_theme_applied
outputs.description: Whether system theme was applied correctly

assertions.message: Light theme must apply correct colors
Dark theme must apply correct colors
System theme must follow OS preference

challenges.name: Desktop: Settings Toggles (Line Numbers, Auto-Save, Animations)
challenges.description: Toggle line numbers, auto-save, and animations settings. Verify each toggle takes immediate effect in the editor UI.

steps.description: Click the Settings navigation button to open the settings panel
Verify the settings panel rendered with all toggle options
Click the line numbers toggle to turn it off
Switch to Files view to verify the editor state
Verify line number gutter is no longer visible in the editor
Return to Settings to re-enable line numbers
Click the line numbers toggle to turn it back on
Switch to Files view to verify line numbers reappeared
Verify line number gutter is visible again in the editor
Return to Settings for auto-save toggle test
Click the auto-save toggle to enable automatic saving
Verify the auto-save status label shows 'enabled'
Click the auto-save toggle to disable it again
Verify the auto-save status label shows 'disabled'
Click the animations toggle to disable UI animations
Verify animations are disabled (no transition effects)
Click the animations toggle to re-enable UI animations
Verify animations are enabled again
Capture a screenshot of the settings panel with all toggles in their final state

outputs.name: line_numbers_toggled
outputs.description: Whether line numbers toggle took effect

outputs.name: auto_save_toggled
outputs.description: Whether auto-save toggle took effect

outputs.name: animations_toggled
outputs.description: Whether animations toggle took effect

assertions.message: Line numbers must toggle on and off
Auto-save must toggle on and off
Animations must toggle on and off

challenges.name: Desktop: Preview Mode Toggle
challenges.description: Open a Markdown document, switch from editor to preview mode, verify rendered HTML output, switch back to editor, and verify content is intact.

steps.description: Double-click the test Markdown file in the file browser to open it
Wait for the Markdown content to fully load in the editor
Verify the editor shows raw Markdown source with # heading syntax
Capture a screenshot of the editor mode showing raw Markdown
Click the Preview button in the toolbar to switch to preview mode
Wait for the Markdown to be rendered as HTML in the preview area
Verify the preview area is visible and displaying rendered content
Verify the rendered HTML contains an <h1> tag for the Markdown heading
Verify the rendered HTML contains <strong> tags for bold text
Verify the rendered HTML contains <li> tags for list items
Capture a screenshot of the preview mode showing rendered HTML
Click the Editor button to switch back from preview to editor mode
Wait for the editor area to reappear with raw Markdown source
Verify the editor content is intact after the preview round-trip

outputs.name: preview_rendered
outputs.description: Whether preview mode rendered HTML correctly

outputs.name: html_structure_valid
outputs.description: Whether HTML contains expected tags

outputs.name: editor_restored
outputs.description: Whether switching back to editor preserved content

assertions.message: Preview mode must render formatted HTML
HTML must contain correct structural tags
Editor content must be intact after preview round-trip

challenges.name: Desktop: Keyboard Shortcuts
challenges.description: Test all primary keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+N (new file), Ctrl+O (open file), Ctrl+S (save), and Ctrl+Z (undo). Verify each shortcut triggers the correct action.

steps.description: Press Ctrl+N to trigger the 'New File' keyboard shortcut
Verify the new file dialog appeared in response to Ctrl+N
Press Escape to dismiss the new file dialog without creating
Verify the new file dialog was dismissed
Press Ctrl+O to trigger the 'Open File' keyboard shortcut
Verify the file open dialog appeared in response to Ctrl+O
Press Escape to dismiss the open file dialog
Verify the open file dialog was dismissed
Click the editor area to give it focus for typing
Type test content that will be undone by Ctrl+Z
Verify the typed content appears in the editor
Press Ctrl+Z to undo the last typing action
Verify the undo reverted the typed content
Type new content to test the save shortcut
Press Ctrl+S to save the current document
Wait for the save indicator to confirm the file was saved to disk
Capture a screenshot after all keyboard shortcut tests completed

outputs.name: ctrl_n_works
outputs.description: Whether Ctrl+N opened new file dialog

outputs.name: ctrl_o_works
outputs.description: Whether Ctrl+O opened file open dialog

outputs.name: ctrl_z_works
outputs.description: Whether Ctrl+Z undid the last action

outputs.name: ctrl_s_works
outputs.description: Whether Ctrl+S saved the file

assertions.message: Ctrl+N must open new file dialog
Ctrl+O must open file open dialog
Ctrl+Z must undo the last action
Ctrl+S must save the current file

challenges.name: Desktop: All Navigation Paths
challenges.description: Navigate through all top-level navigation destinations: Files, Todo, QuickNote, and Settings. Verify each view renders its expected content.

steps.description: Click the 'Files' navigation button in the sidebar
Verify the file browser panel is displayed with directory listing
Verify the editor area is displayed alongside the file browser
Capture a screenshot of the Files navigation view
Click the 'Todo' navigation button in the sidebar
Wait for the Todo view to render with the task list
Verify the Todo task list panel is displayed
Capture a screenshot of the Todo navigation view
Click the 'QuickNote' navigation button in the sidebar
Wait for the QuickNote editor to render
Verify the QuickNote editor area is displayed
Capture a screenshot of the QuickNote navigation view
Click the 'Settings' navigation button in the sidebar
Wait for the Settings panel to render with all options
Verify the Settings panel is displayed with configuration options
Click 'Files' again to verify returning to the initial view works
Verify the file browser panel is restored after a full navigation cycle

outputs.name: files_view
outputs.description: Whether Files view rendered correctly

outputs.name: todo_view
outputs.description: Whether Todo view rendered correctly

outputs.name: quicknote_view
outputs.description: Whether QuickNote view rendered correctly

outputs.name: settings_view
outputs.description: Whether Settings view rendered correctly

outputs.name: return_to_files
outputs.description: Whether returning to Files view worked

assertions.message: Files view must render file browser and editor
Todo view must render task list
QuickNote view must render editor
Settings view must render settings panel
Must return to Files view cleanly

challenges.name: Desktop: Format Info Dialog
challenges.description: Open a file in a recognized format, open the format info dialog, and verify it displays the correct format name, supported extensions, and feature list.

steps.description: Double-click the test Markdown file to open it in the editor
Wait for the file to load and the format to be detected
Verify the format indicator in the status bar shows 'Markdown'
Click the format info button to open the format details dialog
Wait for the format info dialog to appear
Verify the format info dialog is rendered on screen
Verify the dialog shows 'Markdown' as the format name
Verify the dialog lists .md as a supported extension
Verify the dialog shows a non-empty list of supported features
Capture a screenshot of the format info dialog with all details
Click the close/dismiss button on the format info dialog
Verify the format info dialog was dismissed and is no longer visible

outputs.name: dialog_opened
outputs.description: Whether the format info dialog opened

outputs.name: format_info_correct
outputs.description: Whether the format info was accurate

outputs.name: dialog_closed
outputs.description: Whether the dialog closed cleanly

assertions.message: Format info dialog must open
Format info must show correct name and extensions
Dialog must close when dismiss button is clicked

challenges.name: Desktop: Window Resize and Layout Adaptation
challenges.description: Resize the application window to small, medium, and large sizes. Verify that the layout adapts correctly, the file browser collapses on small widths, and all content remains accessible.

steps.description: Verify the main window is visible before starting resize tests
Resize the window to full HD (1920x1080) large desktop size
Wait for the layout to complete reflowing at the new size
Verify the file browser sidebar is visible at full HD width
Verify the editor area is visible alongside the file browser
Capture a screenshot of the large (1920x1080) window layout
Resize the window to medium (1024x768) desktop size
Wait for the layout to complete reflowing at medium size
Verify the editor area is still accessible at medium window size
Capture a screenshot of the medium (1024x768) window layout
Resize the window to small (640x480) compact size
Wait for the layout to complete reflowing at small size
Verify the editor area remains accessible even at the smallest window size
Capture a screenshot of the small (640x480) window layout
Restore the window to full HD (1920x1080) to verify recovery
Wait for the layout to reflow back to full width
Verify the file browser sidebar reappears after restoring to full size

outputs.name: large_layout_ok
outputs.description: Whether full layout rendered at 1920x1080

outputs.name: medium_layout_ok
outputs.description: Whether layout adapted at 1024x768

outputs.name: small_layout_ok
outputs.description: Whether layout adapted at 640x480

outputs.name: resize_recovery
outputs.description: Whether restoring to full size recovered full layout

assertions.message: Full layout must render at 1920x1080
Layout must adapt at 1024x768
Layout must adapt at 640x480 with accessible editor
Restoring window size must recover full layout
